title: gastrolobium rigidum
intro: Gastrolobium rigidum, also known as Oxylobium rigidum or the stiff oxylobium, is a shrub native to Western Australia, characterized by its distinctive yellow and purple flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in Australian Aboriginal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Gastrolobium rigidum include its ability to treat wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate pain, as well as its potential to prevent bacterial and fungal infections. Gastrolobium rigidum can be prepared for medicinal use through various herbal preparations, including infusions, tinctures, and salves, although it is essential to consult with a qualified healthcare professional before using it.
